topic: Two strangers realize they've been dating the same person
raw_output:
[story 1]

The lukewarm coffee suddenly tasted like ash in my mouth. I stared at the man across the crowded cafe, his familiar, infuriatingly charming smile plastered on his face. It was the way he tilted his head when he was trying to be witty, the exact cadence of his laugh, even the slight crinkle near his eyes when he was genuinely amused. A cold, hard knot tightened in my chest, constricting my breath. I watched him gesture expansively, recounting a story I had heard three days ago, a story I was certain I had only shared with him once. My jaw clenched so tightly I could feel the muscle vibrating beneath my skin. *This isn't possible.* I pushed my chair back, the scrape of metal on tile sounding deafening in my own ears. I needed air. I needed distance.

[story 2]

"You look like you've seen a ghost," a voice drawled beside me. I didn't turn immediately. I could feel the tension radiating off him, a palpable energy that made my skin prickle. Then I saw it—the way he reached for his water glass, the slight tremor in his hand, the almost imperceptible shift in his posture that mirrored something I’d experienced moments before. My knuckles were white where I gripped my napkin. I finally turned, and the recognition hit me like a physical blow. He looked at me, and in his eyes, I saw a flicker of bewildered recognition, a dawning horror that mirrored the churning resentment rising in my own gut. I stood up abruptly, my voice sharp, cutting through the ambient noise. "What in the world are you doing here?"

[story 3]

I watched from the periphery as he leaned in to whisper something conspiratorial to the woman sitting beside him. The casual intimacy of the gesture felt like a deliberate, calculated insult. Every shared glance, every familiar inflection in his tone, was a fresh spike of irritation. My hands were balled into fists inside my pockets, the fabric digging into my palms. I took a deep, ragged breath, trying to steady the frantic drumming in my chest. When he finally turned toward me, his expression shifted from relaxed engagement to something guarded, something that suggested he was bracing for impact. He opened his mouth, and I could almost hear the words, but all I registered was the sheer, infuriating audacity of the situation.
